ai-w3-e1.answer: Paste Student class. Ask: encapsulation, naming, constructor issues. Fix top 2 findings yourself.
ai-w3-e1.realWorld: Public fields → private + getters is the most common Week 3 win.

ai-w3-e2.answer: List all single-letter variables in your file. Ask AI for better names. Rename in IDE (refactor tool).
ai-w3-e2.realWorld: IDE rename is safer than AI find-replace — catches references.

ai-w3-e3.answer: Ask AI why encapsulation matters — then explain aloud without reading. Add private balance + deposit() to a class.
ai-w3-e3.realWorld: Interview classic: 'Why private fields?' — control validation in one place.

ai-w3-e4.answer: List your classes and fields. Ask for PlantUML or text UML. Draw correct version by hand.
ai-w3-e4.realWorld: Wrong multiplicity on diagram → wrong DB schema later.

ai-w3-e5.answer: Ask AI to find duplicate code blocks. Extract one shared method. Run tests.
ai-w3-e5.realWorld: Copy-pasted validation in 3 methods → one validateAge() helper.

ai-w3-e6.answer: Paste constructors. Ask for improvements: validation, chaining, defaults. Apply one fix.
ai-w3-e6.realWorld: Constructor setting negative age — add guard clause.

ai-w3-e7.answer: Generate JavaDoc for public methods. Edit wrong descriptions. Match team style (@param, @return).
ai-w3-e7.realWorld: JavaDoc is contract for API consumers — worth getting right on libraries.

ai-w3-e8.answer: Ask: 'What design mistakes do you see?' — god class, tight coupling, static abuse. Pick one to fix.
ai-w3-e8.realWorld: Utility class with 20 static methods — split by domain.

ai-w3-e9.answer: Describe two designs; ask AI to compare. You choose with reasoning — AI doesn't know your deadlines.
ai-w3-e9.realWorld: Inheritance vs composition for Employee types — trade-off discussion for interviews.

ai-w3-e10.answer: Ask for SOLID in simple terms with Java examples. Explain one principle without notes.
ai-w3-e10.realWorld: Single Responsibility: UserService saves users; EmailService sends mail — not one class doing both.
